item.title: Version 0.8.0.b4
item.description:
Installation

macOS App (Recommended)

  1. Download TermQ-0.8.0-beta.4.dmg
  2. Open the DMG and drag TermQ.app to your Applications folder
  3. Double-click to launch

Alternative: Zip Archive

  1. Download TermQ-0.8.0-beta.4.zip
  2. Unzip and move TermQ.app to your Applications folder

CLI Tool

The termqcli CLI is bundled inside the app. To install it:

  1. Open TermQ.app
  2. Go to TermQ → Settings (or press ⌘,)
  3. Click Install Command Line Tool

Or manually:

sudo cp /Applications/TermQ.app/Contents/Resources/termqcli /usr/local/bin/termqcli

Checksums

See checksums.txt for SHA-256 checksums of all release artifacts.

What's Changed

  • feat(ui): UX polish, marketplace improvements, and terminal enhancements by @eyelock in https://github.com/eyelock/TermQ/pull/151
  • docs(workflow): Fix worktree post-merge cleanup sequencing by @eyelock in https://github.com/eyelock/TermQ/pull/152

Full Changelog: https://github.com/eyelock/TermQ/compare/v0.8.0-beta.3...v0.8.0-beta.4

item.title: Version 0.8.0.b3
item.description:
Installation

macOS App (Recommended)

  1. Download TermQ-0.8.0-beta.3.dmg
  2. Open the DMG and drag TermQ.app to your Applications folder

See checksums.txt for SHA-256 checksums of all release artifacts.

What's Changed

  • fix: forward-port hotfix v0.7.5 by @eyelock in https://github.com/eyelock/TermQ/pull/147
  • feat(harnesses): Marketplace browser, harness wizard, and install/launch management by @eyelock in https://github.com/eyelock/TermQ/pull/148

Full Changelog: https://github.com/eyelock/TermQ/compare/v0.8.0-beta.2...v0.8.0-beta.3

item.title: Version 0.8.0.b2
item.description:

macOS App (Recommended)

  2. Open the DMG and drag TermQ.app to your Applications folder
  3. Double-click to launch

  2. Unzip and move TermQ.app to your Applications folder

The termqcli CLI is bundled inside the app. To install it:

  1. Open TermQ.app
  2. Go to TermQ → Settings (or press ⌘,)
  3. Click Install Command Line Tool

Or manually:

sudo cp /Applications/TermQ.app/Contents/Resources/termqcli /usr/local/bin/termqcli

Checksums

See checksums.txt for SHA-256 checksums of all release artifacts.

What's Changed

  • ci: Appcast workflow creates PR instead of pushing directly to main by @eyelock in https://github.com/eyelock/TermQ/pull/135
  • fix(dev): Disable Sparkle auto-updater in debug builds by @eyelock in https://github.com/eyelock/TermQ/pull/137
  • chore: Sync tmux TUI rendering fix to develop by @eyelock in https://github.com/eyelock/TermQ/pull/141
  • feat(harnesses): YNH harness sidebar — detect, install, launch, manage by @eyelock in https://github.com/eyelock/TermQ/pull/142

Full Changelog: https://github.com/eyelock/TermQ/compare/v0.8.0-beta.1...v0.8.0-beta.2

  3. Double-click to launch

  2. Unzip and move TermQ.app to your Applications folder

CLI Tool

The termqcli CLI is bundled inside the app. To install it:

  1. Open TermQ.app
  2. Go to TermQ → Settings (or press ⌘,)
  3. Click Install Command Line Tool

Or manually:

sudo cp /Applications/TermQ.app/Contents/Resources/termqcli /usr/local/bin/termqcli

Checksums

See checksums.txt for SHA-256 checksums of all release artifacts.

What's Changed

  • test: Improve test coverage for TermQ module by @eyelock in https://github.com/eyelock/TermQ/pull/123
  • ci: Introduce develop/main branching strategy by @eyelock in https://github.com/eyelock/TermQ/pull/122
  • fix: Hotfix v0.7.3 — security, focus, and Sequoia compatibility by @eyelock in https://github.com/eyelock/TermQ/pull/134

Full Changelog: https://github.com/eyelock/TermQ/compare/v0.7.2...v0.7.3

item.title: Version 0.8.0.b1
item.description:
Installation

macOS App (Recommended)

  1. Download TermQ-0.8.0-beta.1.dmg
  2. Open the DMG and drag TermQ.app to your Applications folder
  3. Double-click to launch

Alternative: Zip Archive

  1. Download TermQ-0.8.0-beta.1.zip
  2. Unzip and move TermQ.app to your Applications folder

CLI Tool

The termqcli CLI is bundled inside the app. To install it:

  1. Open TermQ.app
  2. Go to TermQ → Settings (or press ⌘,)
  3. Click Install Command Line Tool

Or manually:

sudo cp /Applications/TermQ.app/Contents/Resources/termqcli /usr/local/bin/termqcli

Checksums

See checksums.txt for SHA-256 checksums of all release artifacts.

What's Changed

  • test: Improve test coverage for TermQ module by @eyelock in https://github.com/eyelock/TermQ/pull/123
  • ci: Introduce develop/main branching strategy by @eyelock in https://github.com/eyelock/TermQ/pull/122
  • fix(security): Migrate SecureStorage to Data Protection Keychain by @eyelock in https://github.com/eyelock/TermQ/pull/124
  • fix(ui): Scroll tab bar to reveal selected terminal on sidebar jump by @eyelock in https://github.com/eyelock/TermQ/pull/125
  • ci: Skip Build Release job on PRs targeting develop by @eyelock in https://github.com/eyelock/TermQ/pull/127
  • fix(ui): Scroll tab bar to reveal selected terminal on sidebar jump by @eyelock in https://github.com/eyelock/TermQ/pull/128
  • fix(security): Migrate SecureStorage to Data Protection Keychain by @eyelock in https://github.com/eyelock/TermQ/pull/129
  • fix(focus): Preserve tmux pane focus across SwiftUI re-renders by @eyelock in https://github.com/eyelock/TermQ/pull/133
  • fix(ui): Use async panel.begin() for Browse button in path pickers by @eyelock in https://github.com/eyelock/TermQ/pull/132
  • fix(dev): Debug build shows SHA in About panel by @eyelock in https://github.com/eyelock/TermQ/pull/131
  • fix(security): Use file-based key storage in debug builds by @eyelock in https://github.com/eyelock/TermQ/pull/130
  • feat(ui): Git worktree sidebar by @eyelock in https://github.com/eyelock/TermQ/pull/126

Full Changelog: https://github.com/eyelock/TermQ/compare/v0.7.2...v0.8.0-beta.1

item.title: Version 0.7.2
item.description:
Installation

macOS App (Recommended)

  1. Download TermQ-0.7.2.dmg
  2. Open the DMG and drag TermQ.app to your Applications folder
  3. Double-click to launch

Alternative: Zip Archive

  1. Download TermQ-0.7.2.zip
  2. Unzip and move TermQ.app to your Applications folder

CLI Tool

The termqcli CLI is bundled inside the app. To install it:

  1. Open TermQ.app
  2. Go to TermQ → Settings (or press ⌘,)
  3. Click Install Command Line Tool

Or manually:

sudo cp /Applications/TermQ.app/Contents/Resources/termqcli /usr/local/bin/termqcli

Checksums

See checksums.txt for SHA-256 checksums of all release artifacts.

What's Changed

  • ci: Auto-trigger appcast and fix release CI verification by @eyelock in https://github.com/eyelock/TermQ/pull/120
  • fix(window): Prevent focus stealing and ghost windows from MCP URL opens by @eyelock in https://github.com/eyelock/TermQ/pull/121

Full Changelog: https://github.com/eyelock/TermQ/compare/v0.7.1...v0.7.2

item.title: Version 0.7.1
item.description:
What's New in v0.7.1

Bug Fix

  • Fixed window duplication bug — TermQ windows no longer multiply spontaneously. The root cause was using SwiftUI's WindowGroup (which supports multiple instances and state restoration) instead of Window (which enforces exactly one instance). (#119)

Installation

macOS App (Recommended)

  1. Download TermQ-0.7.1.dmg
  2. Open the DMG and drag TermQ.app to your Applications folder
  3. Double-click to launch

Alternative: Zip Archive

  1. Download TermQ-0.7.1.zip
  2. Unzip and move TermQ.app to your Applications folder

CLI Tool

The termqcli CLI is bundled inside the app. To install it:

  1. Open TermQ.app
  2. Go to TermQ → Settings (or press ⌘,)
  3. Click Install Command Line Tool

Or manually:

sudo cp /Applications/TermQ.app/Contents/Resources/termqcli /usr/local/bin/termqcli

Checksums

See checksums.txt for SHA-256 checksums of all release artifacts.

item.title: Version 0.7.0
item.description:
What's New in v0.7.0

Multi-Pane Terminals

Split your terminal into multiple panes within a single card. TermQ now uses tmux control mode to manage pane layout, resize, and navigation — all through native macOS UI.

Headless Mode & MCP Server

Run TermQ without a GUI for automation workflows. The MCP server now supports smart search, card creation, tags, and deletion — enabling AI agents to manage your terminal sessions programmatically.

Environment Variables & Secrets

Store environment variables and encrypted secrets per terminal card. Secrets are protected with macOS Keychain encryption and injected into sessions at launch.

Redesigned Settings

A complete overhaul of the settings UI with tabbed navigation, reusable components, path input fields, tag editors, and per-terminal configuration panels.

Auto-Updates via Sparkle

TermQ now checks for updates automatically and installs them in the background. No more manual downloads — updates are code-signed and notarized.

40 Languages

Full localization across 40 languages, covering all UI strings, settings, and dialogs.

Security Hardening

Per-terminal security controls, MCP command approval, and URL scheme validation to prevent prompt injection and unauthorized access.

Stability & Polish

  • Fixed window duplication and blank terminal bugs caused by macOS window lifecycle edge cases
  • Improved tmux session recovery with persistent dismissal and cross-build filtering
  • Native text selection restored (tmux mouse mode disabled)
  • PTY size synchronization with Auto Layout
  • Single-window enforcement against macOS tab preferences

Installation

macOS App (Recommended)

  1. Download TermQ-0.7.0.dmg
  2. Open the DMG and drag TermQ.app to your Applications folder
  3. Double-click to launch

Alternative: Zip Archive

  1. Download TermQ-0.7.0.zip
  2. Unzip and move TermQ.app to your Applications folder

CLI Tool

The termqcli CLI is bundled inside the app. To install it:

  1. Open TermQ.app
  2. Go to TermQ → Settings (or press ⌘,)
  3. Click Install Command Line Tool

Or manually:

sudo cp /Applications/TermQ.app/Contents/Resources/termqcli /usr/local/bin/termqcli

Checksums

See checksums.txt for SHA-256 checksums of all release artifacts.

  • fix(release): Generate and include Sparkle EdDSA signatures in appcast by @eyelock in https://github.com/eyelock/TermQ/pull/109

Full Changelog: https://github.com/eyelock/TermQ/compare/v0.7.0-beta.5...v0.7.0-beta.6

item.title: Version 0.7.0.b5
item.description:
BROKEN APPCAST SIGNING See https://github.com/eyelock/TermQ/pull/109 for fix

Installation

macOS App (Recommended)

  1. Download TermQ-0.7.0-beta.5.dmg
  2. Open the DMG and drag TermQ.app to your Applications folder
  3. Double-click to launch

Alternative: Zip Archive

  1. Download TermQ-0.7.0-beta.5.zip
  2. Unzip and move TermQ.app to your Applications folder

CLI Tool

The termqcli CLI is bundled inside the app. To install it:

  1. Open TermQ.app
  2. Go to TermQ → Settings (or press ⌘,)
  3. Click Install Command Line Tool

Or manually:

sudo cp /Applications/TermQ.app/Contents/Resources/termqcli /usr/local/bin/termqcli

Checksums

See checksums.txt for SHA-256 checksums of all release artifacts.

What's Changed

  • ci: bump actions/upload-artifact from 4 to 7 by @dependabot[bot] in https://github.com/eyelock/TermQ/pull/97
  • ci: bump dorny/paths-filter from 3 to 4 by @dependabot[bot] in https://github.com/eyelock/TermQ/pull/98
  • fix(tests): Update tests for MCP swift-sdk 0.12.0 compatibility by @eyelock in https://github.com/eyelock/TermQ/pull/101
  • ci: bump actions/configure-pages from 5 to 6 by @dependabot[bot] in https://github.com/eyelock/TermQ/pull/99
  • ci: bump actions/deploy-pages from 4 to 5 by @dependabot[bot] in https://github.com/eyelock/TermQ/pull/100
  • chore(deps): Drop SwiftTerm fork — all fixes now upstream by @eyelock in https://github.com/eyelock/TermQ/pull/102
  • chore(style): Auto-format sources and fix SwiftLint config conflicts by @eyelock in https://github.com/eyelock/TermQ/pull/103
  • docs: Narrative tutorial rewrite with progressive discovery structure by @eyelock in https://github.com/eyelock/TermQ/pull/104
  • chore(.claude): Migrate to skills/agents/commands three-tier architecture by @eyelock in https://github.com/eyelock/TermQ/pull/105
  • fix: Enforce single-window mode against macOS tab preferences by @eyelock in https://github.com/eyelock/TermQ/pull/106
  • feat: Implement tmux control mode multi-pane management by @eyelock in https://github.com/eyelock/TermQ/pull/96

New Contributors

  • @dependabot[bot] made their first contribution in https://github.com/eyelock/TermQ/pull/97

Full Changelog: https://github.com/eyelock/TermQ/compare/v0.7.0-beta.4...v0.7.0-beta.5

What's Changed

  • fix(ui): Fix session recovery dialog appearing on every launch by @eyelock in https://github.com/eyelock/TermQ/pull/116
  • ci: Bump softprops/action-gh-release from 2 to 3 by @dependabot[bot] in https://github.com/eyelock/TermQ/pull/113

Full Changelog: https://github.com/eyelock/TermQ/compare/v0.7.0-beta.9...v0.7.0-beta.10

item.title: Version 0.7.0.b4
item.description:
Installation

macOS App (Recommended)

  1. Download TermQ-0.7.0-beta.4.dmg
  2. Open the DMG and drag TermQ.app to your Applications folder
  3. Double-click to launch

Alternative: Zip Archive

  1. Download TermQ-0.7.0-beta.4.zip
  2. Unzip and move TermQ.app to your Applications folder

CLI Tool

The termqcli CLI is bundled inside the app. To install it:

  1. Open TermQ.app
  2. Go to TermQ → Settings (or press ⌘,)
  3. Click Install Command Line Tool

Or manually:

sudo cp /Applications/TermQ.app/Contents/Resources/termqcli /usr/local/bin/termqcli

Checksums

See checksums.txt for SHA-256 checksums of all release artifacts.

What's Changed

  • feat(ci): Implement aggregate job pattern for path-based CI checks by @eyelock in https://github.com/eyelock/TermQ/pull/82
  • fix: v0.7.0 stabilization - Phase 0 (Build & Launch) by @eyelock in https://github.com/eyelock/TermQ/pull/83
  • fix: Improve keyboard shortcuts and window management by @eyelock in https://github.com/eyelock/TermQ/pull/84
  • fix(settings): Path inputs and LLM prompt injection UX improvements by @eyelock in https://github.com/eyelock/TermQ/pull/85
  • fix(security): Clear secrets from UI when encryption key is reset by @eyelock in https://github.com/eyelock/TermQ/pull/86
  • feat: Show secret counts in encryption key reset warning by @eyelock in https://github.com/eyelock/TermQ/pull/87
  • feat: Headless mode support for CLI and MCP by @eyelock in https://github.com/eyelock/TermQ/pull/88
  • fix: Phase 4 stabilization - CLI/MCP installation and error handling by @eyelock in https://github.com/eyelock/TermQ/pull/89
  • fix: Phase 5 - TMUX features backend selector UX by @eyelock in https://github.com/eyelock/TermQ/pull/90
  • docs: Document branch protection and Claude Code review workflow by @eyelock in https://github.com/eyelock/TermQ/pull/91
  • fix: Repair broken localization in all 40 language files by @eyelock in https://github.com/eyelock/TermQ/pull/92
  • Fix SwiftLint style warnings (228 violations) by @eyelock in https://github.com/eyelock/TermQ/pull/93
  • fix(ci): Deploy appcast files to GitHub Pages for Sparkle updates by @eyelock in https://github.com/eyelock/TermQ/pull/94
  • fix(sparkle): Fix Settings update button and improve release notes formatting by @eyelock in https://github.com/eyelock/TermQ/pull/95

Full Changelog: https://github.com/eyelock/TermQ/compare/v0.7.0-beta.3...v0.7.0-beta.4

item.title: Version 0.7.0.b3
item.description:
Installation

macOS App (Recommended)

  1. Download TermQ-0.7.0-beta.3.dmg
  2. Open the DMG and drag TermQ.app to your Applications folder
  3. Double-click to launch

Alternative: Zip Archive

  1. Download TermQ-0.7.0-beta.3.zip
  2. Unzip and move TermQ.app to your Applications folder

CLI Tool

The termqcli CLI is bundled inside the app. To install it:

  1. Open TermQ.app
  2. Go to TermQ → Settings (or press ⌘,)
  3. Click Install Command Line Tool

Or manually:

sudo cp /Applications/TermQ.app/Contents/Resources/termqcli /usr/local/bin/termqcli

Checksums

See checksums.txt for SHA-256 checksums of all release artifacts.

What's Changed

  • fix(ci): Fix appcast generation broken pipe error by @eyelock in https://github.com/eyelock/TermQ/pull/78
  • fix(ci): Fix appcast generation and add critical admin merge warning by @eyelock in https://github.com/eyelock/TermQ/pull/79

Full Changelog: https://github.com/eyelock/TermQ/compare/v0.7.0-beta.2...v0.7.0-beta.3

item.title: Version 0.7.0.b2
item.description:
Installation

macOS App (Recommended)

  1. Download TermQ-0.7.0-beta.2.dmg
  2. Open the DMG and drag TermQ.app to your Applications folder
  3. Double-click to launch

Alternative: Zip Archive

  1. Download TermQ-0.7.0-beta.2.zip
  2. Unzip and move TermQ.app to your Applications folder

CLI Tool

The termqcli CLI is bundled inside the app. To install it:

  1. Open TermQ.app
  2. Go to TermQ → Settings (or press ⌘,)
  3. Click Install Command Line Tool

Or manually:

sudo cp /Applications/TermQ.app/Contents/Resources/termqcli /usr/local/bin/termqcli

Checksums

See checksums.txt for SHA-256 checksums of all release artifacts.

What's Changed

  • feat(mcp+cli): Add smart search, init commands, tags, and delete tool by @eyelock in https://github.com/eyelock/TermQ/pull/55

Full Changelog: https://github.com/eyelock/TermQ/compare/v0.7.0-beta.1...v0.7.0-beta.2

item.title: Version 0.7.0.b1
item.description:
Installation

macOS App (Recommended)

  1. Download TermQ-0.7.0-beta.1.dmg
  2. Open the DMG and drag TermQ.app to your Applications folder
  3. Double-click to launch

Alternative: Zip Archive

  1. Download TermQ-0.7.0-beta.1.zip
  2. Unzip and move TermQ.app to your Applications folder

CLI Tool

The termqcli CLI is bundled inside the app. To install it:

  1. Open TermQ.app
  2. Go to TermQ → Settings (or press ⌘,)
  3. Click Install Command Line Tool

Or manually:

sudo cp /Applications/TermQ.app/Contents/Resources/termqcli /usr/local/bin/termqcli

Checksums

See checksums.txt for SHA-256 checksums of all release artifacts.

What's Changed

  • test: Improve unit test coverage by @eyelock in https://github.com/eyelock/TermQ/pull/49
  • feat: Add environment variables and secrets management by @eyelock in https://github.com/eyelock/TermQ/pull/51
  • feat(ui): Add TMUX badge for terminals using tmux backend by @eyelock in https://github.com/eyelock/TermQ/pull/52
  • feat(release): Add Developer ID signing and notarization by @eyelock in https://github.com/eyelock/TermQ/pull/53
  • feat(security): Add security hardening for MCP, URL scheme, and per-terminal controls by @eyelock in https://github.com/eyelock/TermQ/pull/50
  • fix(terminal): Fix PTY size synchronization with Auto Layout by @eyelock in https://github.com/eyelock/TermQ/pull/54
  • feat(update): Add Sparkle auto-update framework integration by @eyelock in https://github.com/eyelock/TermQ/pull/56
  • fix(window): Enforce single window to prevent blank terminals by @eyelock in https://github.com/eyelock/TermQ/pull/57
  • fix(ci): Add workflow_dispatch trigger to release workflow by @eyelock in https://github.com/eyelock/TermQ/pull/58
  • ci(claude): Add Claude Code GitHub Workflow by @eyelock in https://github.com/eyelock/TermQ/pull/59
  • fix(window): Replace reactive window closing with proactive prevention by @eyelock in https://github.com/eyelock/TermQ/pull/60
  • docs(claude): Consolidate CLAUDE.md with command file references by @eyelock in https://github.com/eyelock/TermQ/pull/62
  • fix: improve TMUX backend labeling consistency by @eyelock in https://github.com/eyelock/TermQ/pull/63
  • fix: preserve tmux sessions when deleting cards and improve recovery UX by @eyelock in https://github.com/eyelock/TermQ/pull/64
  • feat: add global default working directory and backend settings by @eyelock in https://github.com/eyelock/TermQ/pull/65
  • ci(claude): Add persistent settings and comprehensive workflow documentation by @eyelock in https://github.com/eyelock/TermQ/pull/66
  • feat: Add reusable UI components for settings improvements (PR #1/8) by @eyelock in https://github.com/eyelock/TermQ/pull/67
  • feat: Add SharedToggle component and reorganize settings tabs (PR #2) by @eyelock in https://github.com/eyelock/TermQ/pull/70
  • docs: improve workflow documentation visibility and enforceability by @eyelock in https://github.com/eyelock/TermQ/pull/69
  • PR #3: Environment Tab Integration by @eyelock in https://github.com/eyelock/TermQ/pull/71
  • feat: Complete Settings UI improvements (PR #4/8) - Tab navigation and tags editor by @eyelock in https://github.com/eyelock/TermQ/pull/72
  • feat: apply PathInputField and SharedToggle to Terminal settings (PR #5/8) by @eyelock in https://github.com/eyelock/TermQ/pull/73
  • feat: improve terminal editor with Prompts tab and better components (PR #6/8) by @eyelock in https://github.com/eyelock/TermQ/pull/74
  • PR #7: Final Polish - StatusIndicator & Settings View Extraction by @eyelock in https://github.com/eyelock/TermQ/pull/75
  • PR #8: Fix Badge Wrapping on Terminal Cards (Final PR) by @eyelock in https://github.com/eyelock/TermQ/pull/76

Full Changelog: https://github.com/eyelock/TermQ/compare/v0.6.1...v0.7.0-beta.1

item.title: Version 0.6.4
item.description:
[!WARNING] The 0.6.x builds are quite unstable, I learned a good lesson here about getting a bit too carried away on how easy it is to ship features with Claude. Shipping, features and fixes that on the surface were working, but when you started trying to use TermQ as your base tool showed up as not quite ready yet. Feel free to try the 0.7.0 beta releases which are focussed on stabilising the app's current features!

Installation

macOS App (Recommended)

  1. Download TermQ-0.6.4.dmg
  2. Open the DMG and drag TermQ.app to your Applications folder
  3. Double-click to launch

Alternative: Zip Archive

  1. Download TermQ-0.6.4.zip
  2. Unzip and move TermQ.app to your Applications folder

CLI Tool

The termqcli CLI is bundled inside the app. To install it:

  1. Open TermQ.app
  2. Go to TermQ → Settings (or press ⌘,)
  3. Click Install Command Line Tool

Or manually:

sudo cp /Applications/TermQ.app/Contents/Resources/termqcli /usr/local/bin/termqcli

Checksums

See checksums.txt for SHA-256 checksums of all release artifacts.

Full Changelog: https://github.com/eyelock/TermQ/compare/v0.6.3...v0.6.4

item.title: Version 0.6.3

  3. Double-click to launch

  2. Unzip and move TermQ.app to your Applications folder

CLI Tool

The termqcli CLI is bundled inside the app. To install it:

  1. Open TermQ.app

item.title: Version 0.6.1
item.description:
Installation

macOS App (Recommended)

  1. Download TermQ-0.6.1.dmg
  2. Open the DMG and drag TermQ.app to your Applications folder
  3. Right-click and select "Open" on first launch (required for unsigned apps)

Alternative: Zip Archive

  1. Download TermQ-0.6.1.zip
  2. Unzip and move TermQ.app to your Applications folder

CLI Tool

The termqcli CLI is bundled inside the app. To install it:

  1. Open TermQ.app
  2. Go to TermQ → Settings (or press ⌘,)
  3. Click Install Command Line Tool

Or manually:

sudo cp /Applications/TermQ.app/Contents/Resources/termqcli /usr/local/bin/termqcli

Checksums

See checksums.txt for SHA-256 checksums of all release artifacts.

What's Changed

  • perf(help): Optimize Help images with pngquant compression by @eyelock in https://github.com/eyelock/TermQ/pull/41
  • fix(help): Fix Bundle.module crash when opening Help in installed app by @eyelock in https://github.com/eyelock/TermQ/pull/42
  • fix(terminal): Fix Restart/Close Session behavior in tab view by @eyelock in https://github.com/eyelock/TermQ/pull/43
  • fix(tabs): Prevent tab header layout shift from spinner by @eyelock in https://github.com/eyelock/TermQ/pull/44
  • fix: Guard focus management during mouse drag to protect text selection by @eyelock in https://github.com/eyelock/TermQ/pull/45
  • fix(help): Reorganize Help topics into sections with headings by @eyelock in https://github.com/eyelock/TermQ/pull/46
  • fix(tmux): Kill tmux sessions when deleting cards and add Kill Session option by @eyelock in https://github.com/eyelock/TermQ/pull/47
  • fix(ux): Add backend restart hint and reorder session menu items by @eyelock in https://github.com/eyelock/TermQ/pull/48

Full Changelog: https://github.com/eyelock/TermQ/compare/v0.6.0...v0.6.1

item.title: Version 0.6.0
item.description:
Installation

macOS App (Recommended)

  1. Download TermQ-0.6.0.dmg
  2. Open the DMG and drag TermQ.app to your Applications folder
  3. Right-click and select "Open" on first launch (required for unsigned apps)

Alternative: Zip Archive

  1. Download TermQ-0.6.0.zip
  2. Unzip and move TermQ.app to your Applications folder

CLI Tool

The termqcli CLI is bundled inside the app. To install it:

  1. Open TermQ.app
  2. Go to TermQ → Settings (or press ⌘,)
  3. Click Install Command Line Tool

Or manually:

sudo cp /Applications/TermQ.app/Contents/Resources/termqcli /usr/local/bin/termqcli

Checksums

See checksums.txt for SHA-256 checksums of all release artifacts.

What's Changed

  • feat(i18n): Add full localization support with 40 languages by @eyelock in https://github.com/eyelock/TermQ/pull/27
  • chore(ci): Consolidate CI/Makefile for local-CI parity by @eyelock in https://github.com/eyelock/TermQ/pull/30
  • refactor: Tech debt cleanup and code quality improvements by @eyelock in https://github.com/eyelock/TermQ/pull/31
  • feat: Add LIVE badge for background terminal sessions by @eyelock in https://github.com/eyelock/TermQ/pull/32
  • feat: Add Kill Terminal option for stuck/unresponsive terminals by @eyelock in https://github.com/eyelock/TermQ/pull/33
  • fix: Improve bell notification reliability and add Dock badge by @eyelock in https://github.com/eyelock/TermQ/pull/34
  • fix(board): Correct same-column card reordering index calculation by @eyelock in https://github.com/eyelock/TermQ/pull/35
  • feat(tabs): Add + button to tab bar for creating new terminals by @eyelock in https://github.com/eyelock/TermQ/pull/36
  • feat: Add duplicate terminal context menu option by @eyelock in https://github.com/eyelock/TermQ/pull/37
  • feat(data): Add backup, restore, and uninstall features by @eyelock in https://github.com/eyelock/TermQ/pull/38
  • feat(tmux): Add tmux backend with control mode, pane rendering, and metadata sync by @eyelock in https://github.com/eyelock/TermQ/pull/39
  • fix(i18n): Fix localization for user-selected languages by @eyelock in https://github.com/eyelock/TermQ/pull/40

Full Changelog: https://github.com/eyelock/TermQ/compare/v0.5.2...v0.6.0

item.title: Version 0.5.2
item.description:
Installation

macOS App (Recommended)

  1. Download TermQ-0.5.2.dmg
  2. Open the DMG and drag TermQ.app to your Applications folder
  3. Right-click and select "Open" on first launch (required for unsigned apps)

Alternative: Zip Archive

  1. Download TermQ-0.5.2.zip
  2. Unzip and move TermQ.app to your Applications folder

CLI Tool

The termq CLI is bundled inside the app. To install it:

  1. Open TermQ.app
  2. Go to TermQ → Settings (or press ⌘,)
  3. Click Install Command Line Tool

Or manually:

sudo cp /Applications/TermQ.app/Contents/Resources/termq /usr/local/bin/

Checksums

See checksums.txt for SHA-256 checksums of all release artifacts.

What's Changed

  • fix(concurrency): Resolve Swift 6 crash in file monitoring by @eyelock in https://github.com/eyelock/TermQ/pull/29

Full Changelog: https://github.com/eyelock/TermQ/compare/v0.5.1...v0.5.2

item.description:

macOS App (Recommended)

  3. Right-click and select "Open" on first launch (required for unsigned apps)

  2. Unzip and move TermQ.app to your Applications folder

CLI Tool

The termq CLI is bundled inside the app. To install it:

  1. Open TermQ.app
  2. Go to TermQ → Settings (or press ⌘,)
  3. Click Install Command Line Tool

Or manually:

sudo cp /Applications/TermQ.app/Contents/Resources/termq /usr/local/bin/

Checksums

See checksums.txt for SHA-256 checksums of all release artifacts.

What's Changed

item.title: Version 0.5.0
item.description:
Installation

macOS App (Recommended)

  1. Download TermQ-0.5.0.dmg
  2. Open the DMG and drag TermQ.app to your Applications folder
  3. Right-click and select "Open" on first launch (required for unsigned apps)

Alternative: Zip Archive

  1. Download TermQ-0.5.0.zip
  2. Unzip and move TermQ.app to your Applications folder

CLI Tool

The termq CLI is bundled inside the app. To install it:

  1. Open TermQ.app
  2. Go to TermQ → Settings (or press ⌘,)
  3. Click Install Command Line Tool

Or manually:

sudo cp /Applications/TermQ.app/Contents/Resources/termq /usr/local/bin/

Checksums

See checksums.txt for SHA-256 checksums of all release artifacts.

What's Changed

  • feat: reorganize Settings into General/Tools tabs by @eyelock in https://github.com/eyelock/TermQ/pull/23
  • feat: reorganize Terminal Editor into General/Advanced tabs by @eyelock in https://github.com/eyelock/TermQ/pull/22
  • feat: add description field to columns by @eyelock in https://github.com/eyelock/TermQ/pull/21
  • feat(mcp): Add MCP Server for LLM Integration by @eyelock in https://github.com/eyelock/TermQ/pull/17
  • feat(env): Add terminal environment variables and termq_get MCP tool by @eyelock in https://github.com/eyelock/TermQ/pull/24
  • feat(ui): add Generate Init Command section to Agents tab by @eyelock in https://github.com/eyelock/TermQ/pull/25
  • fix(mcp): Add file monitoring and MCP status indicator by @eyelock in https://github.com/eyelock/TermQ/pull/26

Full Changelog: https://github.com/eyelock/TermQ/compare/v0.4.4...v0.5.0
